title: Choice lists exceeding 3500 characters emit as external CSV files
description: OC4 hard limit: the choices worksheet of the form template restricts the combination of labels and names to a maximum of 4000 characters. Forms that exceed this fail to upload. DEFAULT: the skill computes total len(label) + len(name) across all rows of each choice list. If a single list exceeds 3500 characters (safety margin below OC's 4000 hard ceiling), emit it as an external CSV file referenced via search() from the survey row, with appearance='minimal autocomplete' applied automatically. Common triggers: medication dictionaries, country/state lists, MedDRA terms, ICD code lists, sponsor DVG term lists, lab test name catalogs. OUTPUT: each externalized list becomes its own CSV file alongside the form xlsx, named {study_id}_{list_name}.csv. Add an entry to the build package manifest. When applied, surface in conventions_applied.external_csv_for_long_lists.{lists_exceeded_threshold, external_csvs_created}.
rationale: OC's 4000-character ceiling on choices-sheet content is a hard build-failure: forms exceeding it don't load. Catching the threshold at build time and converting to an external CSV reference is cheaper than discovering the failure at upload time. The 3500-character safety margin below the 4000 ceiling accounts for slight variance in how the limit is computed (some implementations count cell-level overhead). The auto-applied autocomplete appearance is necessary — external CSV references don't render as native radio/checkbox lists; they require the search()-based autocomplete UI.

flag.message: Choice list ${choice.list_name} — verify total len(label) + len(name) across all rows is under 3500 characters (OC's 4000-char ceiling has a safety margin). If over: emit as external CSV file named {study_id}_{list_name}.csv alongside the form xlsx, replace inline choices with search() reference, and apply appearance='minimal autocomplete'. Add entry to build package manifest.

effect.soft: For each choice list at build time, sum len(label) + len(name) across all rows. If a single list exceeds 3500 characters, externalize to a CSV file named {study_id}_{list_name}.csv. Replace the inline choices block with a search() reference in the survey row, apply appearance='minimal autocomplete' to the field. Add the CSV to the build package manifest under externalized_lists. Sum-character checking from a per-choice context isn't a current DSL primitive — handled in soft. The conventions_applied counters track lists_exceeded_threshold (the count) and external_csvs_created (the list of file names).

history.note: Imported from conventions.md §11 during Phase B.1d. First convention in the new `build_artifacts.*` top-level category (rules about build-output artifacts beyond the form xlsx itself — CSV externalizations, manifest entries, packaging-level decisions). Hybrid because the character-count check and the file-emission are build-pipeline concerns the engine doesn't directly perform. A future DSL primitive could expose 'choice_list_char_total' at field/choice scope to make the trigger structured.
